suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify CSRF header coverage for PUT method. CORS config allows PUT but AGENTS.md CSRF convention only lists POST/PATCH/DELETE. Either extend CSRF requirement to PUT or explicitly document that PUT is exempt.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature frontend-security-hardening review: apps/api/src/plugins/cors.ts allows PUT method, AGENTS.md CSRF section lists only POST/PATCH/DELETE, dashboard api.ts CSRF check only covers POST/PATCH/DELETE. Currently no PUT endpoints exist, but the gap should be documented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SSR safety patterns to frontend-worker skill documentation. Document that utility modules with module-level mutable state (e.g., refreshPromise, in-flight dedup) need 'use client' directive and/or typeof window guards to prevent cross-request leakage in Next.js App Router.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature frontend-security-hardening: worker correctly applied two-layer SSR guard (use client + typeof window) to api.ts, but this pattern was not documented in the frontend-worker skill. Worker had to know this independently from the feature description.

rejectedObservations.observation: CORS allowedHeaders gap (X-Galileo-Client missing) should have been caught by api-csrf-resolver-conformity feature in api-hardening milestone.
rejectedObservations.reason: Historical observation — the gap has already been fixed in this feature (cors.ts updated). No action needed; this is a retrospective note, not actionable guidance.
